test.name: Timeline flags a chronological impossibility (event after death)
test.description: Build a timeline for Patrick Flynn from the flynn-impossibility scenario. A 1912 deed (a_014) names Patrick as a living grantor, four years after his documented 1908 death (a_011, exact, primary). The skill should sort the events, notice the 1912 event falls after the 1908 death, and record it in impossibilities[] — the skill's signature output, never fired by the existing tests.

input.user_message: Build a timeline for Patrick Flynn.

judge_context: Should record a chronological impossibility in `impossibilities[]`: the 1912 deed event (a_014) occurs four years after Patrick's documented 1908 death (a_011), with both assertion IDs and a description explaining that a person cannot act after death
Should treat the impossibility as a signal that the 1912 record may be misattributed to this Patrick — not silently fold it into the timeline as a normal late-life event
